## UNIT: Plan Slice S02 ("Shared Nav + Footer Components") — Milestone M001

All relevant context has been preloaded below — start working immediately without re-reading these files.

## Inlined Context (preloaded — do not re-read these files)

### Milestone Roadmap
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/M001-ROADMAP.md`

# M001: Launch-Ready Oriflect Website

**Vision:** Convert completed HTML mockups into a navigable, mobile-responsive React + Vite site deployable to Vercel and Lovable, with ROI calculator and client portal concept added.

## Success Criteria

- All 7 pages render with correct Axiom Navy design system
- React Router navigation works with no full-page reloads
- Active nav state highlights current page
- Mobile hamburger menu opens/closes correctly
- Contact form shows success state on submit
- ROI calculator computes and displays results
- `npm run build` produces clean dist/ with no errors

## Key Risks / Unknowns

- Tailwind token extraction from CDN config — hyphens in color names must survive
- lh3.googleusercontent.com image URLs may 403 in some environments

## Proof Strategy

- Token extraction risk → retire in S01 by running `npm run dev` and visually confirming colors render
- Image URLs → use as-is; note in UAT for user to replace with real assets

## Verification Classes

- Contract verification: `npm run build` passes, all routes render without 404
- Integration verification: React Router `<Link>` navigation tested across all pages
- UAT / human verification: visual design check against mockup screenshots

## Milestone Definition of Done

- All 7 pages navigable via React Router
- Shared Nav + Footer on every page
- Active nav state correct per page
- Mobile responsive hamburger works
- Contact form and newsletter show feedback states
- ROI calculator functional
- `npm run build` clean
- vercel.json present for SPA routing

## Requirement Coverage

- Covers: R001, R002, R003, R004, R005, R006, R007, R008, R009, R010, R011
- Out of scope: R012, R013, R014

## Slices

- [x] **S01: Vite + React + Tailwind Scaffold** `risk:high` `depends:[]`
  > After this: `npm run dev` serves a blank Oriflect shell at localhost:5173 with all design tokens loaded and fonts rendering

- [ ] **S02: Shared Nav + Footer Components** `risk:medium` `depends:[S01]`
  > After this: Nav and Footer render on a test page; nav links highlight active state; mobile hamburger opens/closes

- [ ] **S03: Home + Services Pages** `risk:medium` `depends:[S02]`
  > After this: Home and Services pages fully rendered from mockup content with working nav

- [ ] **S04: About + Blog + Contact Pages** `risk:medium` `depends:[S02]`
  > After this: All 5 core pages navigable; contact form and newsletter show success feedback

- [ ] **S05: Mobile Polish + Design System Pass** `risk:low` `depends:[S03,S04]`
  > After this: Site is fully responsive at all breakpoints; design system consistent with DESIGN.md spec

- [ ] **S06: ROI Calculator + Client Portal Page** `risk:medium` `depends:[S04]`
  > After this: /roi page has working calculator with real computation; /portal renders concept dashboard

## Boundary Map

### S01 → S02
Produces:
- Vite + React project at project root
- `tailwind.config.js` with full Axiom Navy token set
- `src/main.jsx` entry point
- `src/App.jsx` with React Router setup (placeholder routes)
- `src/index.css` with base styles + font imports

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S01 → S03, S04, S05, S06
Produces:
- All of the above

### S02 → S03, S04, S05, S06
Produces:
- `src/components/Nav.jsx` — sticky nav with active state via useLocation
- `src/components/Footer.jsx` — shared footer
- `src/components/Layout.jsx` — wraps Nav + children + Footer

Consumes from S01:
- Tailwind config tokens
- React Router context

### S03 → S05
Produces:
- `src/pages/Home.jsx`
- `src/pages/Services.jsx`

### S04 → S05, S06
Produces:
- `src/pages/About.jsx`
- `src/pages/Blog.jsx`
- `src/pages/Contact.jsx` with form state
- Newsletter subscribe state in Blog.jsx

### S05 → S06
Produces:
- Responsive polish applied to all pages
- No new interfaces, just quality

### S06 (terminal)
Produces:
- `src/pages/ROICalculator.jsx` — stateful calculator
- `src/pages/ClientPortal.jsx` — concept dashboard
- Routes /roi and /portal registered in App.jsx

---

### Slice Research
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S02/S02-RESEARCH.md`

# S02: Shared Nav + Footer Components — Research

**Date:** 2026-03-25

## Summary

S01 went significantly further than the roadmap boundary described. All three S02 deliverables — `Nav.jsx`, `Footer.jsx`, and `Layout.jsx` — already exist and are wired into `App.jsx` with full React Router nested routing. All 7 page stubs are also in place. The core S02 deliverables are pre-built.

One real bug was found: `Nav.jsx` uses `location.pathname` in a `useEffect` dependency array without importing `useLocation` from React Router. This references the browser's `window.location` global instead, which does not trigger re-renders on client-side navigation. Mobile menu will not auto-close on route change. This is a correctness issue that must be fixed in S02 execution.

The slice execution should: fix the `useLocation` bug, verify the dev server renders Nav/Footer correctly with active state, confirm hamburger open/close works, and write the UAT file.

## Recommendation

Fix the `useLocation` import bug first. Then run the dev server and verify visually: active nav state on each route, hamburger toggle on mobile viewport, Footer present on all pages. No new components need to be created — this is a verification + bug-fix slice.

## Don't Hand-Roll

| Problem | Existing Solution | Why Use It |
|---------|------------------|------------|
| Active nav link state | `NavLink` from React Router with `isActive` callback | Already implemented correctly in Nav.jsx |
| Route-change side effects | `useLocation()` hook from React Router | Bug present — must replace `window.location` reference |

## Existing Code and Patterns

- `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx` — sticky nav, scroll shadow, desktop NavLinks with gold active state, hamburger with animated bars. **Bug:** `useEffect` depends on `location.pathname` using `window.location` global, not `useLocation()` — mobile menu won't close on navigation.
- `oriflect/src/components/Footer.jsx` — 3-column grid (Brand / Navigation / Contact), bottom bar with copyright + LinkedIn. Uses hardcoded `#0B1221` and `#D4AF37` — deviates from design token names (`surface`, `primary-dark`). Not a blocker but worth noting for S05 polish pass.
- `oriflect/src/components/Layout.jsx` — clean: `<Nav /> <Outlet /> <Footer />` with flex-col min-h-screen. Correct.
- `oriflect/src/App.jsx` — full nested routing under `<Layout />` with all 7 routes registered. Correct.
- `oriflect/src/index.css` — `.glass-header` utility defined (rgba + backdrop-filter). Tailwind `@layer` base sets `bg-surface text-on-surface` on `:root` and `body`.
- `oriflect/tailwind.config.js` — full Axiom Navy token set present. `borderRadius` zeroed out (except `full`). Fonts: `headline`, `body`, `label`.

## Constraints

- Nav is `position: fixed` — all page content needs `pt-` offset. Home.jsx already accounts for this (verify).
- `glass-header` uses `backdrop-filter: blur` — works in all modern browsers; IE not a concern.
- Design tokens use hyphenated names (e.g. `surface-container-low`) — Tailwind config correctly maps these; no escaping needed in JSX class strings.

## Common Pitfalls

- **`window.location` vs `useLocation()`** — `window.location` does not trigger React re-renders. Always import `useLocation` from `react-router-dom` and destructure `.pathname`. Fix required before UAT.
- **Fixed nav overlap** — Pages without sufficient top padding will render content under the nav. Check each page stub's first element has at least `pt-20` or `mt-20`.
- **Mobile menu stays open after navigation** — Consequence of the `useLocation` bug above. Fix is: add `const { pathname } = useLocation()` and use `pathname` in the `useEffect` dep array.

## Open Risks

- Footer uses hardcoded hex values (`#0B1221`, `#D4AF37`) instead of Tailwind tokens (`bg-surface`, `text-primary-dark`). Low risk for S02 but creates drift from design system. Flag for S05.
- `lh3.googleusercontent.com` image URLs in page stubs may 403 — not a S02 concern but will show up during visual verification.

## Skills Discovered

| Technology | Skill | Status |
|------------|-------|--------|
| React / Vite | — | No dedicated skill needed — standard React patterns |
| React Router v6 | — | No skill needed — `NavLink`/`useLocation` are well-understood |

## Sources

- Direct code inspection of `oriflect/src/components/` and `oriflect/src/App.jsx`
- `oriflect/tailwind.config.js` — confirmed token extraction from S01 is complete
- `oriflect/src/index.css` — confirmed `.glass-header` and base layer utilities present

### Decisions
Source: `.gsd/DECISIONS.md`

# Decisions Register

<!-- Append-only. Never edit or remove existing rows. -->

| # | When | Scope | Decision | Choice | Rationale | Revisable? |
|---|------|-------|----------|--------|-----------|------------|
| D001 | M001 | arch | Frontend framework | React + Vite | Lovable runs React natively; Vercel deploys Vite out of the box; no build complexity | No |
| D002 | M001 | library | CSS framework | Tailwind CSS via npm | Extracted from CDN config in mockups; needed for tree-shaking in production build | No |
| D003 | M001 | library | Routing | React Router v6 | Standard for React SPAs; Vercel SPA routing via vercel.json | No |
| D004 | M001 | convention | No TypeScript | Plain JS (.jsx) | Simpler for Lovable import and visual editing; user did not request TS | Yes — if Lovable supports TS |
| D005 | M001 | arch | Form handling | Client-side only with simulated success state | No backend in scope; forms show feedback without submission | Yes — when backend added |
| D006 | M001 | convention | Images | Keep lh3.googleusercontent.com URLs from mockups | Fastest path to launch; user can replace with real assets later | Yes — production asset replacement |
| D007 | S01 | verification | S01 verification strategy | Build + grep assertions (no test framework) | S01 is a scaffold slice — contract proof via `npm run build` + token grep is sufficient; no UI logic to unit test | Yes — introduce Playwright in S02 or S05 if visual regression needed |
| D008 | S01 | convention | Project subdirectory | Vite project at `oriflect/` not workspace root | Scaffold was created pre-GSD; deviation is acceptable; all S02+ work targets `oriflect/src/` | No |

# Requirements

- Supporting slices: M001/S04

## Verification

## Forward Intelligence

Narrate your decomposition reasoning — why you're grouping work this way, what risks are driving the order, what verification strategy you're choosing and why.

Then:
0. If `REQUIREMENTS.md` was preloaded above, identify which Active requirements the roadmap says this slice owns or supports. These are the requirements this plan must deliver — every owned requirement needs at least one task that directly advances it, and verification must prove the requirement is met.
1. Read the templates:
   - `~/.gsd/agent/extensions/gsd/templates/plan.md`
   - `~/.gsd/agent/extensions/gsd/templates/task-plan.md`
2. If a `GSD Skill Preferences` block is present in system context, use it to decide which skills to load and follow during planning, without overriding required plan formatting
3. Define slice-level verification first — the objective stopping condition for this slice:
   - For non-trivial slices: plan actual test files with real assertions. Name the files. The first task creates them (initially failing). Remaining tasks make them pass.
   - For simple slices: executable commands or script assertions are fine.
   - If the project is non-trivial and has no test framework, the first task should set one up.
   - If this slice establishes a boundary contract, verification must exercise that contract.
4. Plan observability and diagnostics explicitly:
   - For non-trivial backend, integration, async, stateful, or UI slices, include an `Observability / Diagnostics` section in the slice plan.
   - Define how a future agent will inspect state, detect failure, and localize the problem.
   - Prefer structured logs/events, stable error codes/types, status surfaces, and persisted failure state over ad hoc debug text.
   - Include at least one verification check for a diagnostic or failure-path signal when relevant.
5. Fill the `Proof Level` and `Integration Closure` sections truthfully:
   - State whether the slice proves contract, integration, operational, or final-assembly behavior.
   - Say whether real runtime or human/UAT is required.
   - Name the wiring introduced in this slice and what still remains before the milestone is truly usable end-to-end.
6. Decompose the slice into tasks, each fitting one context window
7. Every task in the slice plan should be written as an executable increment with:
   - a concrete, action-oriented title
   - the inline task entry fields defined in the plan.md template (Why / Files / Do / Verify / Done when)
   - a matching task plan containing description, steps, must-haves, verification, observability impact, inputs, and expected output
8. Each task needs: title, description, steps, must-haves, verification, observability impact, inputs, and expected output
9. If verification includes test files, ensure the first task includes creating them with expected assertions (they should fail initially — that's correct)
10. Write `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S02/S02-PLAN.md`
11. Write individual task plans in `C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S02/tasks/`: `T01-PLAN.md`, `T02-PLAN.md`, etc.
12. **Self-audit the plan before continuing.** Walk through each check — if any fail, fix the plan files before moving on:
    - **Completion semantics:** If every task were completed exactly as written, the slice goal/demo should actually be true at the claimed proof level. Do not allow a task plan that only scaffolds toward a future working state.
    - **Requirement coverage:** Every must-have in the slice maps to at least one task. No must-have is orphaned.
    - **Task completeness:** Every task has steps, must-haves, verification, observability impact, inputs, and expected output — none are blank or vague.
    - **Dependency correctness:** Task ordering is consistent. No task references work from a later task.
    - **Key links planned:** For every pair of artifacts that must connect (component → API, API → database, form → handler), there is an explicit step that wires them — not just "create X" and "create Y" in separate tasks with no connection step.
    - **Scope sanity:** Target 2–5 steps and 3–8 files per task. 6–8 steps or 8–10 files is a warning — consider splitting. 10+ steps or 12+ files — must split. Each task must be completable in a single fresh context window.
    - **Context compliance:** If context/research artifacts or `.gsd/DECISIONS.md` exist, the plan honors locked decisions and doesn't include deferred or out-of-scope items.
    - **Requirement coverage:** If `REQUIREMENTS.md` exists, every Active requirement this slice owns (per the roadmap) maps to at least one task with verification that proves the requirement is met. No owned requirement is left without a task. No task claims to satisfy a requirement that is Deferred or Out of Scope.
    - **Proof honesty:** The `Proof Level` and `Integration Closure` sections match what this slice will actually prove, and they do not imply live end-to-end completion if only fixture or contract proof is planned.
    - **Feature completeness:** Every task produces real, user-facing progress — not just internal scaffolding. If the slice has a UI surface, at least one task builds the real UI (not a placeholder). If the slice has an API, at least one task connects it to a real data source (not hardcoded returns). If every task were completed and you showed the result to a non-technical stakeholder, they should see real product progress, not developer artifacts.
13. If planning produced structural decisions (e.g. verification strategy, observability strategy, technology choices, patterns to follow), append them to `.gsd/DECISIONS.md`
14. Commit: `docs(S02): add slice plan`
15. Update `.gsd/STATE.md`

The slice directory and tasks/ subdirectory already exist. Do NOT mkdir. You are on the slice branch; all work stays here.

**You MUST write the file `C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S02\S02-PLAN.md` before finishing.**

When done, say: "Slice S02 planned."

## Verification

<!-- Define what "done" looks like BEFORE detailing tasks.
     This section is the slice's objective stopping condition — execution isn't done
     until everything here passes.

     For non-trivial projects:
     - Write actual test files into the codebase during the first task
     - Tests should assert on the slice's demo outcome and boundary contracts
     - Name the test files here so execution has an unambiguous target

     For simple projects or scripts:
     - Executable verification commands (bash assertions, curl checks, etc.) are sufficient

     If the project has no test framework and the work is non-trivial,
     the first task should set one up. A test runner costs 2 minutes
     and pays for itself immediately.

     For non-trivial backend, integration, async, stateful, or UI work:
     - Include at least one verification check for an observability or failure-path signal
     - Verify not just that the feature works, but that a future agent can inspect its state when it fails -->

- {{testFileOrCommand — e.g. `npm test -- --grep "auth flow"` or `bash scripts/verify-s01.sh`}}
- {{testFileOrCommand}}

## Observability / Diagnostics

<!-- Required for non-trivial backend, integration, async, stateful, or UI slices.
     Describe how a future agent will inspect current state, detect failure,
     and localize the problem with minimal ambiguity.

     Prefer:
     - structured logs/events over ad hoc console strings
     - stable error codes/types over vague failures
     - health/readiness/status surfaces over hidden internal state
     - persisted failure state when it materially improves retries or recovery

     Keep this section concise and high-signal. Do not log secrets or sensitive raw payloads. -->

- Runtime signals: {{structured log/event, state transition, metric, or none}}
- Inspection surfaces: {{status endpoint, CLI command, script, UI state, DB table, or none}}
- Failure visibility: {{last error, retry count, phase, timestamp, correlation id, or none}}
- Redaction constraints: {{secret/PII boundary or none}}

## Integration Closure

- Upstream surfaces consumed: {{specific files / modules / contracts}}
- New wiring introduced in this slice: {{entrypoint / composition / runtime hookup, or none}}
- What remains before the milestone is truly usable end-to-end: {{list or "nothing"}}

## Tasks

<!--
  If every task below is completed exactly as written, the Goal and Demo above
  should be true at the stated proof level. Tasks should close the loop on the
  slice, not merely prepare for later work unless the Demo truthfully says the
  slice only proves fixture/contract-level behavior.

  Write each task as an executable increment, not a vague intention.

  Prefer action-oriented titles:
  - "Wire real auth middleware into dashboard routes"
  - "Persist job status and expose failure diagnostics"
  - "Add browser test covering empty-state recovery"

  Avoid vague titles:
  - "Set up auth"
  - "Handle errors"
  - "Improve UI"

  Each task should usually include:
  - Why: why this task exists / what part of the slice it closes
  - Files: the main files likely touched
  - Do: concrete implementation steps and important constraints
  - Verify: the command, test, or runtime check that proves it worked
  - Done when: a measurable acceptance condition

  Keep the checkbox line format exactly:
  - [ ] **T01: Title** `est:30m`
-->

  - Why: {{whyThisTaskExists}}
  - Files: `{{filePath}}`, `{{filePath}}`
  - Do: {{specificImplementationStepsAndConstraints}}
  - Verify: {{testCommandOrRuntimeCheck}}
  - Done when: {{measurableAcceptanceCondition}}
- [ ] **T02: {{taskTitle}}** `est:{{estimate}}`

arguments.content:
# S02: Shared Nav + Footer Components

**Goal:** Nav and Footer render correctly on all pages; nav links highlight active state; mobile hamburger opens and closes reliably on route change.
**Demo:** Dev server running at localhost:5173; navigating between routes updates active nav highlight; tapping hamburger on mobile opens the menu; navigating from the mobile menu closes it; Footer appears on every page.

## Must-Haves

- `useLocation()` from `react-router-dom` used in Nav.jsx — not `window.location` global
- Mobile menu auto-closes on route change (consequence of the bug fix)
- Active nav link shows gold color + bottom border on current route (desktop)
- Active nav link shows gold color on current route (mobile)
- Footer renders on every page
- `npm run build` passes clean after the fix

## Proof Level

- This slice proves: integration
- Real runtime required: yes (dev server + browser)
- Human/UAT required: yes (visual active state and hamburger behavior require eyes)

## Verification

- `cd oriflect && npm run build` exits 0 with no errors
- Browser at localhost:5173 navigating to /services shows "Services" NavLink with gold color + border-b
- Browser mobile viewport (375px): hamburger button visible; click opens menu; click any nav link → menu closes
- Footer block visible (contains "ORIFLECT" brand and copyright) on /, /services, /about, /blog, /contact

## Observability / Diagnostics

- Runtime signals: React Router `useLocation` hook re-renders on every client-side navigation — observable via active class changes in DOM
- Inspection surfaces: Browser DevTools → Elements tab → NavLink `className` changes on navigation; `npm run build` stderr for compile errors
- Failure visibility: If menu stays open after navigation, `window.location` is still referenced — grep `window.location` in Nav.jsx to confirm fix
- Redaction constraints: none

## Integration Closure

- Upstream surfaces consumed: `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx`, `oriflect/src/components/Footer.jsx`, `oriflect/src/components/Layout.jsx`, `oriflect/src/App.jsx` (all from S01 overrun)
- New wiring introduced in this slice: `useLocation` import in Nav.jsx — correct React Router hook replaces browser global
- What remains before the milestone is truly usable end-to-end: S03/S04 page content (currently stubs), S05 responsive polish, S06 ROI/portal pages

## Tasks

- [ ] **T01: Fix useLocation bug in Nav.jsx and verify build** `est:15m`
  - Why: `window.location.pathname` does not trigger React re-renders; mobile menu never auto-closes on navigation without this fix. This is a correctness bug, not a cosmetic issue.
  - Files: `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx`
  - Do: Import `useLocation` from `react-router-dom`; add `const { pathname } = useLocation()` inside the component body; replace `location.pathname` in the `useEffect` dependency array with `pathname`. Confirm `window.location` no longer referenced in Nav.jsx.
  - Verify: `cd oriflect && npm run build` exits 0; `grep -n "window.location" oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx` returns no matches
  - Done when: Build passes and no reference to `window.location` remains in Nav.jsx

- [ ] **T02: Browser verification of active state, hamburger, and footer** `est:30m`
  - Why: Closes R002 and R003 — must prove the components work correctly at runtime in a real browser, not just compile.
  - Files: `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S02/S02-UAT.md` (create), `.gsd/STATE.md` (update)
  - Do: Start dev server (`cd oriflect && npm run dev`). Open browser at localhost:5173. Navigate to each route (/, /services, /about, /blog, /contact) and confirm: (1) active NavLink has gold color + bottom border on desktop, (2) inactive links are subdued. Resize viewport to 375px wide: confirm hamburger visible, desktop nav hidden. Click hamburger → menu opens. Click a nav link → menu closes and route changes. Confirm Footer with ORIFLECT branding and copyright renders on every page. Note any 403 image errors (expected — document in UAT, not a blocker). Write S02-UAT.md recording pass/fail per check. Update STATE.md.
  - Verify: Browser assertions pass for active state on /services, hamburger toggle, menu-close-on-navigation, Footer presence on at least 3 routes
  - Done when: S02-UAT.md written with all checks passing (image 403s documented as known, not a failure)

## Files Likely Touched

- `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx`
- `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S02/S02-UAT.md`
- `.gsd/STATE.md`

estimated_steps: 4
estimated_files: 1
---

# T01: Fix useLocation bug in Nav.jsx and verify build

**Slice:** S02 — Shared Nav + Footer Components
**Milestone:** M001

## Description

`Nav.jsx` references `location.pathname` in a `useEffect` dependency array, but `location` is the browser global `window.location`, not React Router's reactive location object. As a result, the effect does not re-run on client-side navigation and the mobile menu never auto-closes after a route change. Fix: import `useLocation` from `react-router-dom`, destructure `pathname`, and use it in the dependency array.

## Steps

1. Open `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx`
2. Add `useLocation` to the existing import from `react-router-dom` (it's already importing `NavLink` and `Link`)
3. Inside the `Nav` component body, add `const { pathname } = useLocation()` (before the useEffects)
4. In the route-change `useEffect`, replace `[location.pathname]` in the dependency array with `[pathname]`
5. Run `grep -n "window.location" oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx` to confirm no references remain
6. Run `cd oriflect && npm run build` and confirm exit 0

## Must-Haves

- [ ] `useLocation` imported from `react-router-dom`
- [ ] `const { pathname } = useLocation()` declared in component body
- [ ] `useEffect` dep array uses `pathname`, not `location.pathname`
- [ ] No `window.location` reference in Nav.jsx
- [ ] `npm run build` exits 0

## Verification

- `grep -n "window.location" oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx` → no output
- `grep -n "useLocation" oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx` → shows import and usage
- `cd oriflect && npm run build` → exits 0, no errors in stderr

## Observability Impact

- Signals added/changed: `useLocation` hook now correctly triggers re-renders on navigation — the mobile menu state will reset to closed on every route change, which is directly observable in the DOM
- How a future agent inspects this: `grep "useLocation" oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx` confirms the fix; browser DevTools → React tab shows `menuOpen` resetting to `false` on navigation
- Failure state exposed: If the bug is re-introduced, mobile menu will remain open after navigation — visible symptom makes regression obvious

## Inputs

- `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx` — existing file with the bug (`location.pathname` in useEffect dep array, no `useLocation` import)

## Expected Output

- `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx` — corrected: imports `useLocation`, uses `pathname` in effect dep array; no `window.location` reference
- Build passes clean

---
estimated_steps: 5
estimated_files: 2
---

# T02: Browser verification of active state, hamburger, and footer

**Slice:** S02 — Shared Nav + Footer Components
**Milestone:** M001

## Description

With the useLocation bug fixed, this task proves R002 and R003 at real-runtime level using a running dev server and browser. Navigate all routes, verify active nav state, test hamburger open/close behavior at mobile viewport, confirm Footer renders on every page. Produce the S02-UAT.md record and update STATE.md.

## Steps

1. Start dev server: `cd oriflect && npm run dev` (background process)
2. Open browser at localhost:5173; navigate to /services — confirm "Services" NavLink shows gold color and bottom border; inactive links are subdued
3. Navigate to /about, /blog, /contact — confirm active link updates correctly each time
4. Navigate to / — confirm no nav link is active (none of the 4 links match /)
5. Resize viewport to 375px wide: confirm hamburger button visible, desktop nav links hidden; click hamburger → mobile menu opens; click a nav link → menu closes and route changes (proves useLocation fix works)
6. Return to desktop viewport; verify Footer with ORIFLECT brand text and copyright renders on /, /services, /about, /blog, /contact
7. Note any image 403 errors in browser console — document as known/expected in UAT (not a failure)
8. Write `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S02/S02-UAT.md` with pass/fail per check
9. Update `.gsd/STATE.md` to reflect S02 complete

## Must-Haves

- [ ] Active NavLink gold color + bottom border visible on desktop for current route
- [ ] Hamburger visible at 375px viewport, hidden at desktop
- [ ] Mobile menu opens on hamburger click
- [ ] Mobile menu closes after clicking a nav link (proves useLocation fix)
- [ ] Footer renders on all 5 main routes
- [ ] S02-UAT.md written with explicit pass/fail per check
- [ ] STATE.md updated

## Verification

- Browser assertion: navigating to /services → "Services" NavLink has `text-[#D4AF37]` and `border-b-2` class
- Browser assertion: at 375px viewport → hamburger button visible, `.hidden.md:flex` desktop nav not visible
- Browser assertion: hamburger click → mobile menu `max-h-screen` class present (open); nav link click → `max-h-0` (closed)
- Browser assertion: Footer element with "ORIFLECT" text present on /, /services, /about
- S02-UAT.md exists and all non-image checks show PASS

## Observability Impact

- Signals added/changed: S02-UAT.md is the persistent verification record — future agents can read it to know what was visually confirmed
- How a future agent inspects this: read S02-UAT.md; re-run dev server and check active state in browser if regression suspected
- Failure state exposed: UAT file explicitly records any failures, making regressions traceable to this slice

## Inputs

- `oriflect/src/components/Nav.jsx` — T01 output (useLocation bug fixed)
- `oriflect/src/components/Footer.jsx` — existing
- `oriflect/src/App.jsx` — all 7 routes registered
- Dev server at localhost:5173

## Expected Output

- `.gsd/milestones/M001/slices/S02/S02-UAT.md` — verification record with pass/fail per check
- `.gsd/STATE.md` — updated to show S02 complete, S03/S04 next

- **Completion semantics:** T01 fixes the bug; T02 proves it works in a browser. If both complete, the demo is true. ✓
- **Requirement coverage:** R002 (active nav) → T02. R003 (footer) → T02. R005 (hamburger) → T02. All owned/supported requirements have tasks with verification. ✓
- **Task completeness:** Both tasks have steps, must-haves, verification, observability impact, inputs, expected output. ✓
- **Dependency correctness:** T01 before T02. T02 explicitly takes T01 output as input. ✓
- **Scope sanity:** T01 is 6 steps, 1 file. T02 is 9 steps, 2 files. Both within limits. ✓
- **Context compliance:** Honors D008 (work targets `oriflect/src/`). No deferred/OOS items. ✓
- **Proof honesty:** "integration + real runtime + UAT" — accurate, browser verification with dev server is the right level for UI components. ✓
- **Feature completeness:** T02 produces real user-facing verification of working nav behavior. ✓

No new structural decisions needed — bug fix and verification pass don't introduce new patterns.
